name: AI Safety, Alignment & Ethics for Engineers
description: A practical guide to building trustworthy AI systems. Covers alignment concepts, safety techniques, bias and fairness, red-teaming, regulatory landscape (EU AI Act, NIST framework), and engineering practices for responsible AI deployment.
targetAudience: Engineers, tech leads, and engineering managers responsible for shipping AI features in consumer or enterprise products

steps.title: AI Safety & Alignment: Core Concepts
steps.description: Understand the alignment problem, goal misgeneralization, reward hacking, and why building safe AI is fundamentally hard. Survey alignment research approaches.

substeps.title: Study: Alignment problem and why it matters now
Study: RLHF, Constitutional AI, and RLAIF approaches
Discuss: Which failure modes are relevant to your product

steps.title: Bias, Fairness & Representation
steps.description: Understand how bias enters AI systems through data, labels, and architecture. Learn fairness definitions (demographic parity, equalized odds) and mitigation techniques.

substeps.title: Study: Types of bias in training data and model behavior
Audit: Bias analysis on a public model or dataset
Build: Fairness metrics for your team's specific use case
Verify: Document and publish your fairness findings

steps.title: Red-Teaming & Adversarial Testing
steps.description: Systematically probe your AI systems for safety failures. Run structured red-team exercises, document failure modes, and build a remediation roadmap.

substeps.title: Study: Red-teaming methodology and toolkits
Build: Red-team prompt library for your use case
Test: 20 adversarial attacks against your system
Document: Vulnerability report with severity ratings
Verify: Retest after mitigations — confirm fixes hold

steps.title: Content Safety & Output Guardrails
steps.description: Design and implement content safety guardrails: input filters, output classifiers, human escalation paths, and monitoring for harmful content in production.

substeps.title: Study: Available content safety APIs and classifiers
Design: Content policy for your product's risk tolerance
Build: Input/output filtering pipeline
Build: Human escalation workflow for edge cases
Verify: False positive/negative rates meet policy thresholds

steps.title: Regulatory Landscape & Compliance
steps.description: Navigate AI regulation: EU AI Act risk categories, NIST AI RMF, GDPR implications for AI, and emerging sector-specific rules. Build a compliance checklist for your team.

substeps.title: Study: EU AI Act high-risk categories and obligations
Study: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
Assess: Which regulatory tier your product falls under
Build: Compliance checklist and documentation template
Verify: Legal/compliance team reviews and signs off
